infobox.value: (21 October 1918 – 6 February 1995) was chairman of [Porsche AG](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") between 1972 and 1980. During his first time in Zuffenhausen, in the mid 1950s, he was in charge of developing the [Porsche Type 547 racing engine](/wiki/Porsche_547_engine "Porsche 547 engine"), which became known as the "Fuhrmann engine".

first_paragraph: **Dr. Ernst Fuhrmann** (21 October 1918 – 6 February 1995) was chairman of [Porsche AG](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") between 1972 and 1980. During his first time in Zuffenhausen, in the mid 1950s, he was in charge of developing the [Porsche Type 547 racing engine](/wiki/Porsche_547_engine "Porsche 547 engine"), which became known as the "Fuhrmann engine".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

German businessman

| Ernst Fuhrmann | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | 21 October 1918  [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna"), [Austria-Hungary](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary") |
| Died | 6 February 1995(1995-02-06) (aged 76)  [Teufenbach](/wiki/Teufenbach "Teufenbach"), [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria") |
| Occupation | Mechanical Engineer |

**Dr. Ernst Fuhrmann** (21 October 1918 – 6 February 1995) was chairman of [Porsche AG](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") between 1972 and 1980. During his first time in Zuffenhausen, in the mid 1950s, he was in charge of developing the [Porsche Type 547 racing engine](/wiki/Porsche_547_engine "Porsche 547 engine"), which became known as the "Fuhrmann engine".

Fuhrmann born in the last days of [Austria-Hungary](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary"), but died as a German citizen. Fuhrmann attended primary school and [secondary school](/wiki/Gymnasium_(school) "Gymnasium (school)") in Vienna. Between 1936 and 1941 he attended Vienna technical university [TU Wien](/wiki/TU_Wien "TU Wien"). During the [war](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") he undertook his military service, and from 1943 worked at AEG in Berlin, researching ultrasound and infrared devices.[[1]](#cite_note-1) By 1947 he was working for Porsche, initially at the [facility in Gmünd, Austria](/wiki/Gm%C3%BCnd,_Carinthia "Gmünd, Carinthia"), later moving to [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart").[[2]](#cite_note-AutoMotoruSport1978-2) In 1950 he married his wife Elfriede. That same year he received his Doctorate of Mechanical Engineering from Prof. Richter at TU Wien for his *study of [valvetrains](/wiki/Valvetrain "Valvetrain") in high-speed [internal combustion engines](/wiki/Internal_combustion_engines "Internal combustion engines").*

In 1952/53 he provided significant input to the development of the [Porsche Type 547 engine](/wiki/Porsche_547_engine "Porsche 547 engine"), which became known as the "Fuhrmann engine". It was first used in the [Porsche 550](/wiki/Porsche_550 "Porsche 550"). This air-cooled four-cylinder [boxer engine](/wiki/Flat_engine#Boxer_engine "Flat engine") has [two overhead camshafts](/wiki/Overhead_camshaft#Dual_overhead_camshaft "Overhead camshaft") on each of the [Volkswagen Beetle](/wiki/Volkswagen_Beetle "Volkswagen Beetle")-style two cylinder banks, driven by [bevel gears](/wiki/Bevel_gears "Bevel gears") on horizontal shafts in the same concept as the pre-war [Wolseley](/wiki/Wolseley_Motors "Wolseley Motors") OHC valvetrain (which had a racing success in [MG K3](/wiki/MG_K-type#K3 "MG K-type")), and had a built-up [Hirth](/wiki/Hirth_joint "Hirth joint")-type crankshaft on [roller bearings](/wiki/Roller_bearing "Roller bearing") instead of a typical one-piece crankshaft on split [bearing metal](/wiki/Bearing_metal "Bearing metal").

In 1956 Fuhrmann left Porsche and took over responsibility for development with the car parts company [Goetze](/wiki/Federal-Mogul "Federal-Mogul"). In 1962 he was appointed to the board of directors.[[2]](#cite_note-AutoMotoruSport1978-2)

Fuhrmann returned to Porsche in 1971 as technical director. From 1972 to 1980 he served as chairman of the board at Porsche, which by then had become a [joint-stock](/wiki/Joint-stock_company "Joint-stock company") (German "Aktiengesellschaft" - (AG)) company.[[2]](#cite_note-AutoMotoruSport1978-2) In 1977 he was granted an honorary professorship by the [Vienna University of Technology](/wiki/Vienna_University_of_Technology "Vienna University of Technology").[[3]](#cite_note-AutoMotorundSport197721-3) In the 1970s, the [Porsche 911](/wiki/Porsche_911 "Porsche 911"), then a nearly decade old design, was supposed to be replaced by front engined water cooled [Porsche 924](/wiki/Porsche_924 "Porsche 924") and [Porsche 928](/wiki/Porsche_928 "Porsche 928"). Fuhrmann supported the development of 911 variants like the Carrera RS, the 930 Turbo and the [Porsche 935](/wiki/Porsche_935 "Porsche 935"), but planned to discontinue the 911 range in the 1980s.

In 1980 Porsche suffered its first money-losing year in history, much of the problem due to falling sales in the U.S. and lackluster takeup of their new designs, 924 and 928. Porsche removed long-time CEO Ernst Fuhrmann and started looking for a replacement. He was succeeded at Porsche in 1981 by German-American [Peter W. Schutz](/wiki/Peter_W._Schutz "Peter W. Schutz") who *saved the 911*.
